text:
You know? I (more or less) didn't mind this particular earrape. I'm mostly against them because some poopers tend to overuse earraping, but cranking up the volume and oversaturating the colors alone does not make a joke, and guys like you seem to understand this pretty well.

I know it's done for emphasis, but I noticed some "earrapes" weren't as annoying as others.

Then I noticed DaThings1 and you use a different technique for earrapes: on top of always justifying them with a decent joke/punchline, instead of cranking up the volume so that the audio suffers from severe clipping you actually add reverb to it, and try not to clip it too much. This gives the proper sensation of EMPHASIS and loudness without frying your brain. It's a bit like when you speak really close to a microphone/megaphone. It feels more natural.

The volume cranking is also relatively mild, but still noticeable. It doesn't throw you off at all.
